title: avena bromoides subsp. pruinosa
intro: Avena bromoides subsp. pruinosa, commonly known as wild oat or oat grass, is a subspecies of grass belonging to the Avena genus. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional European folk medicine, to treat digestive issues, insomnia, and restlessness. The plant is rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving sleep quality, and supporting digestive health. Avena bromoides subsp. pruinosa can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, often combined with other herbs to enhance its effects.
